title: rubus discoideus
intro: Rubus discoideus, also known as the discoideus bramble or desert raspberry, is a type of flowering shrub in the rose family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing due to its high content of antioxidants and tannins. The health benefits of Rubus discoideus include its potential to reduce the risk of certain cancers, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection against free radicals. Rubus discoideus can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various forms of consumption and application.
